name: La Atalaya – running circular by Mora Encantada trail
name_original: La Atalaya – running circular por sendero de la Mora Encantada
region_intro: Jogging around Mancha Alta offers diverse landscapes across this interprovincial region of Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. The area is characterized by vast plains, significant wetland ecosystems like "Mancha Húmeda," and river basins that create varied terrain. Extensive fields of cereals, olive trees, and vineyards define many routes, providing tranquil and aesthetically pleasing backdrops for running. Best jogging routes around Mancha Alta The most popular jogging route is La Atalaya – running circular by Mora Encantada trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route includes some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Tarancón loop from Tarancón, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route offers a loop around the town of Tarancón. Local runners also love the Trail to Cerro Mojón – Tarancón loop from Tarancón, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail featuring a climb to Cerro Mojón, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Mancha Alta is defined by vast plains, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes with vineyards and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more moderate routes with some elevation. The routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Mancha Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Mancha Alta?
A: Komoot offers over 25 diverse running routes in Mancha Alta, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. The majority of these, 24 to be exact, are rated as moderate difficulty, with one easy and one difficult option also available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Mancha Alta?
A: Mancha Alta offers a diverse landscape for runners. You'll find vast plains, significant wetland ecosystems known as "Mancha Húmeda" with lagoons and streams, and river basins that create varied terrain. Many routes also traverse extensive fields of cereals, olive trees, and vineyards, providing tranquil and aesthetically pleasing backdrops.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Mancha Alta?
A: While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy running route available in Mancha Alta, which could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous option. The generally flat nature of the plains also offers many accessible paths.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Mancha Alta?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain, including those in Mancha Alta, are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations, particularly in protected natural areas like the Tablas de Daimiel National Park or Ruidera Lakes Natural Park, where specific rules might apply to protect wildlife.

Q: When is the best time of year to go jogging in Mancha Alta?
A: Mancha Alta experiences a continental climate with distinct seasons. Spring is particularly recommended for jogging, as the landscape comes alive with vivid colors. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are best, while winters can be chilly but offer crisp, clear days.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running in Mancha Alta?
A: The region is rich in cultural and natural sights. You might encounter the iconic Windmills of Don Quixote, particularly around Consuegra or Campo de Criptana. For historical interest, consider routes near medieval castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5917394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Uclés and Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include the unique reddish clay formations of the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña, or the serene wetlands of the "Mancha Húmeda".

Q: Are there circular running paths available in Mancha Alta?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Mancha Alta are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014673257/la-atalaya-running-circular-by-mora-encantada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Atalaya – running circular by Mora Encantada trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.5 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.4 km circular path.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Mancha Alta?
A: The running routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from vast plains to river valleys and agricultural landscapes, as well as the tranquil and aesthetically pleasing backdrops provided by vineyards and olive groves.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes in Mancha Alta provide glimpses of the region's rich history. While not directly on a running path, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle of Puebla de Almenara&lt;/a&gt; can be enjoyed from nearby trails. The region is also dotted with iconic windmills and medieval castles, which often serve as scenic backdrops for runs across the plains.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near natural parks?
A: While the guide focuses on routes within Mancha Alta, the broader region of Castilla-La Mancha is home to significant natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parque_nacional_de_las_Tablas_de_Daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parque_natural_de_las_Lagunas_de_Ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruidera Lakes Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for their wetlands and interconnected lakes, offer picturesque routes for nature enthusiasts, though specific jogging routes within these parks would need to be explored separately.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Mancha Alta?
A: Among the listed routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón loop from Estación de Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, covering approximately 14.8 kilometers (9.2 miles). This moderate route offers a substantial distance for those looking for a longer run.
tips: Very close to the town of Torrejoncillo del Rey is the hill of La Atalaya with its peak above 1000 meters above sea level. The panoramic views are very beautiful in all directions, you can clearly appreciate the silhouette of the San Bartolomé hill and the valleys of the Cigüela and Valdepalomar rivers that come together very close to here in the town of Horcajada de la Torre.
